title: useRef
description: DOM refs and mutable values that survive renders.

options: A mutable .current — mutating it doesn't trigger re-render
questions.explanation: For values that persist without causing renders (timers, prev value, DOM).

questions.prompt: Attaching a ref to a DOM element:
questions.explanation: React will assign the DOM node to inputRef.current after commit.

questions.code:
const r = useRef(0);
function click() { r.current++; }
options: No — mutating a ref doesn't re-render
questions.explanation: Use state if you need to render. Refs are silent.

questions.prompt: When to use useRef instead of useState:
options: Value used only in handlers/effects, not in JSX
questions.explanation: Refs skip renders and are perfect for latest-value tracking.

options: ref is now a normal prop for function components — no forwardRef needed
Only class components can hold refs
questions.explanation: Modern React lets you accept `ref` directly.
